title: Sharing, Fairness, and Being a Good Citizen
subject: social_studies

content.text:
Good communities are built by good citizens — people who do their part to help and treat others fairly!

🤝 What is Citizenship?
Citizenship means being a member of a community — and doing your part to make it better. A CITIZEN:
• Follows rules and laws
• Respects others
• Helps their neighbors
• Takes care of shared spaces (parks, schools, libraries)
• Speaks up for fairness

⚖️ What is Fairness?
Fairness means everyone gets what they need and are treated with respect — it does not always mean everyone gets the EXACT same thing.

Example: If one student needs extra time on a test because of a learning difference, giving them extra time is FAIR — everyone gets what they need to succeed.

💛 Acts of Kindness and Service:
• Picking up trash in a park
• Helping a neighbor carry groceries
• Including a classmate who is left out
• Donating food to a food bank
• Volunteering at a shelter

🌍 Global Citizens:
We are not just citizens of our neighborhood or country — we are global citizens! Our choices affect people around the world:
• Buying fair-trade products supports farmers in other countries
• Reducing waste helps keep the planet clean for everyone

✨ Children as Citizens:
You don't have to be an adult to be a good citizen! You can be kind, fair, honest, and helpful every single day.

The world gets better when each person decides to make it better.

diagrams.alt: Illustration of children doing acts of good citizenship in their community

readingPassage.title: The Empty Lunch Table
readingPassage.text:
Every day at lunch, a girl named Amelia sat alone at the far end of the cafeteria. She had moved to the school three weeks ago and hadn't made any friends yet.

Jaden noticed her one Tuesday. He watched her eat quietly, looking at her tray.

"Why doesn't anyone sit with her?" he asked his friend Mia.

"I don't know," Mia said. "She seems shy."

Jaden thought about it for the rest of lunch. When the bell rang, he made a decision.

The next day, he walked over to Amelia's table before she sat down. "Hey — do you want to sit with us?"

Amelia looked surprised. Then she smiled. "Sure."

At the table, Jaden's friends were a little quiet at first. But soon they found out that Amelia loved drawing, just like Mia did. By the end of lunch, they were laughing and planning an art project together.

That night, Jaden's teacher sent home a note: "Jaden showed exceptional kindness today by including a new student."

Jaden's mom read it and hugged him. "You didn't do it for a note, though, did you?"

Jaden shook his head. "No. I just knew what it felt like to be the new kid."

His mom smiled. "That's what makes it real kindness."

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: Why was Amelia sitting alone at lunch?

options.text: She preferred to sit alone
She had moved to the school recently and hadn't made friends yet
She was in trouble with the teacher
She arrived late to lunch every day

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Read the very first paragraph of the story.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: What did Jaden decide to do the next day?

options.text: Tell the teacher about Amelia
Invite Amelia to sit with his group
Sit alone too
Ask Amelia to help with homework

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Find the paragraph where Jaden makes a decision and walks over to Amelia.

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: What did Jaden and Amelia have in common with Mia?

options.text: They all loved soccer
They all liked reading
Amelia and Mia both loved drawing
They were all new students

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Read the paragraph about what happened at the lunch table — what did they discover about Amelia?

comprehensionQuestions.prompt: Why did Jaden's mom say his kindness was 'real'?

options.text: Because he got a good grade
Because his teacher told him to
Because he did it from empathy, not for a reward
Because everyone saw him

comprehensionQuestions.hint: Read the last exchange between Jaden and his mom — why does she say it's real kindness?

questions.prompt: What does it mean to be a good citizen?

options.text: Being famous in your community
Following rules, respecting others, and helping your community
Winning every competition
Having the most money
Everyone gets exactly the same thing
The strongest person wins
Everyone gets what they need and is treated with respect
Only adults make decisions

questions.prompt: Which of these is an act of good citizenship?

options.text: Throwing trash in the park
Ignoring someone who needs help
Picking up litter and keeping shared spaces clean
Taking more than your share
Traveling to every country
Understanding that their choices can affect people around the world
Speaking many languages
Living in a big city
No, only adults can be citizens
Only if they are class president
Yes — by being kind, fair, honest, and helpful every day
Only if they volunteer at big events
